import pandas as pd
import altair as alt

# Create a clean dataset with complete data for visualization
df_clean = _db.execute("""
    SELECT 
        date,
        real_gdp,
        cpi,
        fed_funds_rate,
        unemployment_rate,
        gdp_growth_pct,
        cpi_inflation_rate,
        treasury_10y,
        yield_spread
    FROM us_macro_quarterly_fred_126b4cea
    WHERE fed_funds_rate IS NOT NULL 
      AND unemployment_rate IS NOT NULL
      AND gdp_growth_pct IS NOT NULL
      AND cpi_inflation_rate IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY date
""").df()

print(f"Clean data shape: {df_clean.shape}")
print(f"Date range: {df_clean['date'].min()} to {df_clean['date'].max()}")

# Convert date to string for Altair
df_clean['date_str'] = pd.to_datetime(df_clean['date']).dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')

# Create GDP Growth vs Inflation scatter plot with trend
chart1 = alt.Chart(df_clean).mark_point(opacity=0.6, size=50).encode(
    x=alt.X("gdp_growth_pct:Q", title="GDP Growth (%)"),
    y=alt.Y("cpi_inflation_rate:Q", title="Inflation Rate (%)"),
    color=alt.Color("fed_funds_rate:Q", title="Fed Funds Rate (%)", legend=None),
    tooltip=["date_str:N", "gdp_growth_pct:Q", "cpi_inflation_rate:Q", "fed_funds_rate:Q"]
).properties(
    title="GDP Growth vs Inflation (colored by Fed Funds Rate)",
    width=500,
    height=400
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)

# Add trend line
trend = alt.Chart(df_clean).mark_line(color='red', strokeWidth=2).encode(
    x=alt.X("gdp_growth_pct:Q", title="GDP Growth (%)"),
    y=alt.Y("cpi_inflation_rate:Q", title="Inflation Rate (%)")
).transform_regression(
    "gdp_growth_pct",
    "cpi_inflation_rate"
)

chart1 = chart1 + trend

save_chart_vegalite(chart1, "GDP Growth vs Inflation Relationship")

import pandas as pd
import altair as alt

# Time series of key indicators
df_ts = _db.execute("""
    SELECT 
        date,
        real_gdp,
        cpi,
        fed_funds_rate,
        unemployment_rate,
        gdp_growth_pct,
        cpi_inflation_rate,
        treasury_10y,
        yield_spread
    FROM us_macro_quarterly_fred_126b4cea
    WHERE date IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY date
""").df()

df_ts['date_str'] = pd.to_datetime(df_ts['date']).dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')

# Create time series chart with multiple indicators
chart2 = alt.Chart(df_ts).mark_line().encode(
    x=alt.X("date_str:T", title="Date"),
    y=alt.Y("value:Q", title="Value"),
    color=alt.Color("metric:N", title="Indicator")
).transform_pivot(
    value="value",
    name="metric",
    aggregate="mean",
    field="metric",
    op="argmax"
).properties(
    title="Key Economic Indicators Over Time",
    width=600,
    height=400
)

# Simpler approach - just plot GDP and Inflation
chart2 = alt.Chart(df_ts).mark_line().encode(
    x=alt.X("date_str:T", title="Date"),
    y=alt.Y("gdp_growth_pct:Q", title="GDP Growth (%)"),
    color=alt.value("steelblue")
).properties(
    title="GDP Growth Rate Over Time",
    width=600,
    height=300
)

chart3 = alt.Chart(df_ts).mark_line().encode(
    x=alt.X("date_str:T", title="Date"),
    y=alt.Y("cpi_inflation_rate:Q", title="Inflation Rate (%)"),
    color=alt.value("firebrick")
).properties(
    title="Inflation Rate Over Time",
    width=600,
    height=300
)

# Combine GDP and Inflation on dual axis
chart2 = alt.Chart(df_ts).mark_line(color="steelblue").encode(
    x=alt.X("date_str:T", title="Date"),
    y=alt.Y("gdp_growth_pct:Q", title="GDP Growth (%)")
)

chart3 = alt.Chart(df_ts).mark_line(color="firebrick").encode(
    x=alt.X("date_str:T", title="Date"),
    y=alt.Y("cpi_inflation_rate:Q", title="Inflation Rate (%)")
)

combined = alt.layer(chart2, chart3).properties(
    title="GDP Growth vs Inflation Over Time",
    width=600,
    height=400
)

save_chart_vegalite(combined, "GDP Growth vs Inflation Over Time")

args.code:
import pandas as pd
import altair as alt

# Get data for unemployment and Fed Funds Rate relationship
df_unemp = _db.execute("""
    SELECT 
        date,
        fed_funds_rate,
        unemployment_rate
    FROM us_macro_quarterly_fred_126b4cea
    WHERE fed_funds_rate IS NOT NULL 
      AND unemployment_rate IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY date
""").df()

df_unemp['date_str'] = pd.to_datetime(df_unemp['date']).dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')

# Phillips Curve: Unemployment vs Inflation
df_phillips = _db.execute("""
    SELECT 
        date,
        unemployment_rate,
        cpi_inflation_rate
    FROM us_macro_quarterly_fred_126b4cea
    WHERE unemployment_rate IS NOT NULL 
      AND cpi_inflation_rate IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY date
""").df()

df_phillips['date_str'] = pd.to_datetime(df_phillips['date']).dt.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')

# Phillips Curve scatter plot
base = alt.Chart(df_phillips).mark_point(opacity=0.6, size=50).encode(
    x=alt.X("unemployment_rate:Q", title="Unemployment Rate (%)"),
    y=alt.Y("cpi_inflation_rate:Q", title="Inflation Rate (%)"),
    color=alt.Color("date_str:T", title="Date", legend=None),
    tooltip=["date_str:N", "unemployment_rate:Q", "cpi_inflation_rate:Q"]
)

trend = alt.Chart(df_phillips).mark_line(color='red', strokeWidth=2).encode(
    x=alt.X("unemployment_rate:Q"),
    y=alt.Y("cpi_inflation_rate:Q")
).transform_regression("unemployment_rate", "cpi_inflation_rate")

chart_phillips = (base + trend).properties(
    title="Phillips Curve: Unemployment vs Inflation",
    width=500,
    height=400
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)

save_chart_vegalite(chart_phillips, "Phillips Curve: Unemployment vs Inflation")
